gaps: A circuit-appeal statpack cut keyed to appeal events (affirm/reverse/dismiss rates by circuit and pro-se status); the by-court cut is dominated by 'other' because merits labels like 'affirmed' fall through normalize_disposition
fedcourts query returned no ca11 trademark priors and no captions/dates on the historical rows it did return, limiting comparability
Guidance in the event definition on how an affirmance maps onto the Disposition enum (denied vs other) would tighten disposition-label agreement between predictors and reconcilers
helpful: Snapshot's verbatim district-court order text (entry 23) — carried the whole merits picture
notes: MCP was configured but deliberately unused: the snapshot already showed 'Opinion Issued', so any live lookup on this case risked outcome leakage. Corpus ranged reads are logged in retrieval.md.
